text:
It was a windy evening at the sleepy town library. A solitary figure stood by the bookshelf. It was Clara, a retired librarian known for her vast knowledge of detective novels. She was there pouring over a detective manual, her hand flying over the pages.

Just then, something rustled at the window. She peered out. A small, oddly shaped bird fluttered past. Intrigued, Clara closed her book and approached the window. She noticed it was a kestrel—a beautiful bird that seemed strangely out of place in a quiet town.

"I haven’t seen one of those in years," Clara mused aloud. She watched it gracefully dive into a hollow oak tree.

As she stood there, she heard rustling closer. Out from behind the tree marched an old black cat. It sniffed the kestrel hungrily, eyeing it with what seemed like a desire for companionship. Clara chuckled softly.

"You’re not afraid of little birds?" she said.

"No bother
